reason: The text demonstrates that the common people (עם) actively make decisions throughout the narrative, not just the leaders and God. Specifically: (1) The people assemble themselves (וַיִּקָּהֵ֨ל הָעָ֜ם עַֽל־ אַהֲרֹ֗ן) and collectively address Aaron with demands; (2) All the people independently decide to remove their gold earrings (וַיִּתְפָּֽרְקוּ֙ כָּל־ הָעָ֔ם אֶת־ נִזְמֵ֥י הַזָּהָ֖ב); (3) The people decide to eat, drink, and engage in play (וַיֵּשֶׁב הָעָם֙ לֶֽאֱכֹ֣ל וְשָׁת֔וֹ וַיָּקֻ֖מוּ לְצַחֵֽק). While the leaders (Aaron and Moses) and God make significant decisions, the ordinary people also exercise agency in making collective decisions that drive the narrative.
